theme: edge-cache prefetching for a content network
title: Edge Cache: Prefetching Along the Drifting Hot Set
strategies: trivial: best FIXED cache chosen once by total weight over the whole horizon, held forever (== the evaluator baseline) -> ~0.1

greedy: textbook reactive LRU/LFU replay -- admits the C items with the highest just-observed weight[t] at each step; because of the one-step fetch latency this can defend step t+1 at best, so it is always chasing the deterministic drift from behind, missing the leading edge of the hot block every step and churning (evict/refetch) on ids that cyclically return

strong: lookahead-value ranking -- score every item by its linearly-decayed (future weight * miss_cost) over a short lookahead starting at t+1, discount UNcached items by their flat fetch_cost, keep the top-C combined scores; this fills the cache with the NEXT hot ids before they ever miss and only evicts when a costlier upcoming arrival needs the slot, directly exploiting that the drift is deterministic and already visible in the given table
invalid: try to cache the whole catalog every step -> exceeds the hard per-step capacity cap on every instance -> infeasible -> 0
